data.name: A bucket is a versioned snapshot of a repository's packages at a point in time. The primary bucket is the live one served to apt clients.
data.value: A bucket is a versioned snapshot of a repository's packages at a point in time. The primary bucket is the live one served to apt clients.

data.name: Being built by a currently-running Sync job. Will replace the LIVE bucket on success.
data.value: Being built by a currently-running Sync job. Will replace the LIVE bucket on success.

data.name: Buckets allow atomic updates. New packages are built into a fresh bucket, then the primary pointer switches over seamlessly.
data.value: Buckets allow atomic updates. New packages are built into a fresh bucket, then the primary pointer switches over seamlessly.

data.name: Not referenced by any Mirror or Repository. Will be permanently deleted on the next Garbage Collection run.
data.value: Not referenced by any Mirror or Repository. Will be permanently deleted on the next Garbage Collection run.

data.name: Sum of all package file sizes listed in this bucket. Actual disk usage may be lower because CAS (content-addressable storage) deduplicates files shared between buckets.
data.value: Sum of all package file sizes listed in this bucket. Actual disk usage may be lower because CAS (content-addressable storage) deduplicates files shared between buckets.

data.name: This snapshot is actively served to apt clients as the current index.
data.value: This snapshot is actively served to apt clients as the current index.

data.name: Unreferenced buckets (not primary or secondary in any Mirror/Repository) are immediately pruned on the next GC run. In-progress builds are protected because both SyncJobs set SecondaryBucketId immediately upon bucket creation.
data.value: Unreferenced buckets (neither primary nor secondary in any Mirror/Repository) are pruned immediately on the next GC run. In-progress builds are protected because both SyncJobs set SecondaryBucketId immediately upon bucket creation.
